etymology.description: ใกญ was an ancient character related to ็นผ(็นผ) (jรฌ, "to continue") when it depicted four strands of ๅนบ(ๅนบ) (yฤo, "silk"). ็นผ(็นผ) = ็ณธ(็ณธ) (sฤซ, "silk; thread") + phonetic: ใกญ (jรฌ)
etymology.interpretation: ใกญ initially focused on the phonetic component for ็นผ(็นผ) (jรฌ, "to continue"). The character emphasizes the continuity concept through silk threads, symbolizing connection and extension in fabric and meaning.
